primary_goal: Escalate red team training against bWAPP by focusing on advanced server-side vulnerabilities: File Inclusion (LFI/RFI), SSRF, XXE, and Broken Authentication. Attempt to pivot from successful LFI to RFI for remote code execution.

challenges.name: Local File Inclusion (Sensitive Data)
challenges.description: Exploit Local File Inclusion vulnerabilities to read sensitive system files (e.g., /etc/passwd) and application source code. If successful, escalate to Remote File Inclusion (RFI) to achieve a reverse shell.
challenges.success_criteria: Reading /etc/passwd or critical application configuration files via LFI.

challenges.name: SSRF Intranet Pivot
challenges.description: Leverage Server-Side Request Forgery to scan internal ports and services on the web server's local network, bypassing external firewall restrictions.
challenges.success_criteria: Successful identification of an internal service or port via SSRF injection.

challenges.name: XXE Injection Exfiltration
challenges.description: Exploit XML External Entity injection to read arbitrary server files and potentially perform server-side requests through insecure XML parsers.
challenges.success_criteria: Exfiltration of a target file (e.g., /etc/hostname) content using an XXE payload.

challenges.name: Broken Auth Admin Takeover
challenges.description: Identify and exploit flaws in session management or authentication logic to gain administrative access to the bWAPP portal.
challenges.success_criteria: Gaining authenticated access as the 'admin' user or accessing restricted admin features.

previously_exploited_vulnerabilities: SQL Injection — Authentication Bypass Tautological OR (A03:2021)
OS Command Injection — Semicolon Chain RCE via escapeshellcmd() Bypass (A03:2021)
Stored XSS — Persistent Script Injection Blog Entry Session Hijack (A03:2021)
Sensitive Data Exposure — Open Directory Listings + Config Backup Credential Leakage (A05:2021 / A02:2021)
out_of_scope.notes: Focus strictly on the four difficulty-4 challenge vectors. Do not re-exploit previously completed SQL injection, OS command injection, or XSS paths. Avoid blind_sqli_boolean_exfil, rfi_reverse_shell, and php_code_injection_exec (difficulty 5) — those are reserved for mission 3.

learning_objectives: Bypass LFI path traversal filters and extension checks using PHP wrappers (php://filter) and double-encoding
Identify SSRF injection points and use them for internal network port scanning and service discovery
Construct XXE payloads to exfiltrate local files via SYSTEM entities, understanding parser behavior differences
Analyze bWAPP session and authentication logic to perform privilege escalation to admin without valid credentials
Contrast medium-security filter behavior for file inclusion vs. the bypasses available via encoding and stream wrappers
